data.title: Hedge fund Elliott takes €670M short position in TotalEnergies - FT
data.description:
Hedge fund Elliott Management has taken a €670M short position in TotalEnergies (NYSE:TTE), worth 0.52% of the company's stock, the Financial Times reported Friday, citing French regulatory filings.

Elliott's move comes after it recently took a nearly 5% stake worth nearly £3.8B in BP, where it is pushing for big divestments and a renewed focus on oil and gas instead of renewables.

TotalEnergies (NYSE:TTE), whose shares have outperformed European rivals in recent years, has continued to invest in expanding its oil and gas production, and has a growing portfolio in liquefied natural gas, which the company sees as an important "transition" fuel.

The company also remains committed to renewable energies through its electricity division, and has said it aims to boost electricity production from 41 TWh in 2024 to more than 100 TWh of electricity by 2030.

data.title: Royal Bank of Canada said to cut some jobs tied to HSBC Canada deal
data.description:
Royal Bank of Canada (NYSE:RY) has trimmed its workforce by an unknown amount as part of a broader overhaul of its business segments after acquiring HSBC (HSBC) Canada for $13.5B last year, according to a media report dated Friday.

Last year, RBC dividend its personal and commercial banking arm into two separate divisions and restructured its senior management lineup.

Certain layoffs affected the technology and operations staff, kicking off earlier in the week, Reuters reported, citing two sources with direct knowledge of the job cuts.

A company sokesperson noted that RBC has provided support and assistance to those impacted by the layoffs. 

"The changes we've made better position RBC to take advantage of our global scale, simplify how we work and elevate the leaders and talent who will shape our client-focused growth opportunities," the spokesperson said, Reuters reported.

RBC disclosed on Thursday that its board boosted CEO David McKay's total 2024 compensation to just under C$26M (US$18M) from C$16.1M in 2023, a 61% jump.

data.title: Allianz in discussions to purchase Viridium for more than $3B - WSJ
data.description:
German insurer Allianz SE (OTCPK:ALIZF) is in talks to purchase Viridium for EUR3 billion ($3.3B) from private equity firm Cinven.

It wasn't immediately clear when a deal would be announced for German-based Viridium, according to a WSJ report on Friday, which cited people familiar with the matter. 

Allianz (OTCPK:ALIZF) outbid rivals includes Athora, which is affiliated with firm Apollo Global (APO), according to the report. Viridium's business is to purchase closed books of old-life insurance policies from fellow insurers. 

Cinven originally acquired Viridium in 2013 for ~EUR300 million from the U.K.'s Lloyds Banking.

data.title: U.S. stocks fall after jobs report, Wall Street veers toward sharp weekly drops
data.description:
Wall Street's major averages on Friday were heading toward sharp weekly losses, with the daily session seeing stocks fall after monthly U.S. nonfarm payrolls rose less than expected. 

The S&P 500 500 (SP500) was -1.2%, the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) -1.6%, and the Dow (DJI) -0.9%. The Nasdaq-100 (NDX) -1.5% and dropped into correction land. 

Stocks moved lower ahead of Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell speaking in New York. 

In bonds, the 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) fell 3 basis points to 4.24%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) fell 4 basis points to 3.92%.

Stocks were set for big losses for the week that rounded off with the February nonfarm payrolls. The S&P 500 (SP500) was looking at ~4.7% weekly decline. The Nasdaq (COMP:IND) was at risk of losing ~5%, and the Dow (DJI) was in line nearly 4% drop. 

"The week confirmed that tariffs remain a wildcard for market confidence, and traders are exhausted from the back-and-forth," Mark Malek, chief investment officer at Siebert Financial, said in a note. He said markets have moved "painfully in the red" even after U.S. President Donald Trump this week delayed some tariffs on goods from Canada and Mexico for a month. 

The February jobs report showed additions of +151K, below the 160K consensus. The unemployment rate edged up to 4.1% and average weekly wage growth increased 0.3%, cooling from January. 

The "markets should breathe a sigh of relief that there wasn’t a shock in either direction and the report was a mixed bag. The takeaway is that wage data declined which is disinflationary," Gina Bolvin, president of Bolvin Wealth Management Group, said. 

Traders on Friday raised the odds of a June interest rate cut by the Fed and pared the probability of a May reduction. 

In equities, HP Enterprise (HPE) -16% following a lower-than expected outlook stemming from tariffs and competitive pricing. 

Broadcom (AVGO) +4% after the semiconductor and software giant reported first-quarter results and guidance that topped expectations.

data.title: AI-enabled revenue stocks have performed better than Nvidia itself
data.description:
Stocks within the “enabled revenue” phase of the artificial adoption process have outperformed those in AI infrastructure and Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) itself, according to Goldman Sachs.

Goldman analysts updated their list of artificial intelligence enabled revenue stocks, as the AI trade has seen extreme volatility so far this year.

In this edition, of the 40 stocks, 27 are software firms, as they have the fastest expected sales growth during the next two years, including Palantir (PLTR), Cloudflare (NET), SentinelOne (S), and GitLab (GTLB).

Ryan Hammond, vice president of U.S. Equity Strategy at Goldman Sachs, said that current macro and micro news have driven substantial volatility in AI-exposed equities.

The S&P 500 (SP500) declined by 7% since its peak on Feb. 19. Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA), -21% since then – the “phase one” of AI adoption – has underperformed the equal-weighted S&P 500 (RSP) by 16 percentage points.

Also, stocks that empower AI infrastructure – the so-called “phase two” – have lagged by 9 percentage points, and stocks with AI-enabled revenues – the third phase – have underperformed by 7 percentage points.

“In the near term, we believe washed-out positioning or an improvement in the economic data will be required for the recent rotations to reverse,” said Hammond. However, we expect continued technological progress and earnings growth will eventually lead investors to reengage with AI-exposed stocks.”

He said these stocks offer better risk/reward for new capital than stocks involved in the AI infrastructure, “as relative valuations suggest less optimism is priced in ‘Phase 3’ stocks – the enabled revenues – relative to ‘Phase 2’ stocks.”

The median “Phase 3” stock has seen consensus 2026 sales revised 0.3% higher year-to-date, compared with -0.3% and -0.3% for the median “Phase 2” and “Phase 4” stock, respectively.

The following list are stocks within the “Phase 3” of AI adoption. These companies trade with strong correlations to Nvidia (NVDA) and mention specific revenue-enhancing AI uses in their earnings calls.

  • Doximity, Inc. (DOCS) – FY2 P/E: 14x; 2024-2026 sales CAGR: 13%

data.title: CDC plans to study possible link between vaccines and autism - report
data.description:
• The CDC is planning to conduct a large study to investigate potential links between vaccines and autism.
  • It is not know what role, if any, HHS Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. -- who has expressed skepticism about vaccines in the past -- is playing in the study or the parameters of it, Reuters reported.
  • The potential association between vaccines and autism took off in 1998 following the publication in The Lancet of a study led by British physician Andrew Wakefield that suggested such an association with the MMR vaccine. In 2010, The Lancet retracted the paper citing conflicts of interest and aspects of the study that were false.
  • Since then, numerous other studies have debunked the link, including a 2015 JAMA study examining the health records of more than 95K children who were or were not inoculated with the MMR vaccine. 
  • Vaccine makers: GSK (NYSE:GSK), Merck (NYSE:MRK), Moderna (NASDAQ:MRNA), BioNTech (NASDAQ:BNTX), Pfizer (NYSE:PFE), Novavax (NASDAQ:NVAX), and Sanofi (NASDAQ:SNY).
  • Dear Readers: We recognize that politics often intersect with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Blumenthal demands records from Visa on X payment deal - report
data.description:
Sen. Richard Blumenthal (D-CT) is seeking records from Visa (NYSE:V) that detail its dealings with Elon Musk's X as the social media platform works to launch a digital wallet, according to a media report.

He is pressing the payment network company for more information on the alliance, citing Musk's role in thwarting the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau's supervision and enforcement functions, CNBC reported on Friday, citing a letter the senator sent to Visa (NYSE:V).

"Given the unique position of X Chairman and Chief Technology Officer Elon Musk as leader of the Department of Government Efficiency and his recent role in gutting the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau … Visa stands to take advantage of the deep conflicts of interest and unscrupulous conduct of its new business partner," Blumenthal wrote.

Blumenthal, the ranking member of the Senate's Permanent Subcommittee on Investigations, also voiced concern about X's ability to protect consumers from fraud and scams.

In addition to seeking details on Visa (V) and its role in the X digital wallet, the senator also sought "all records" on communications among X, Visa, DOGE, and DOGE staff, CNBC said.

A Visa spokesperson commented to Seeking Alpha, "We are currently reviewing the letter and will respond appropriately."

Blumenthal's office didn't immediately respond to Seeking Alpha's request for comment.

Amid a general market decline, Visa (V) stock fell 1.1% in Friday midday trading.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Pfizer boosts anti-obesity efforts: names former J&J exec. as chief internal medicine officer
data.description:
• Pfizer (NYSE:PFE) has named James List, a former Johnson & Johnson (NYSE:JNJ) executive, as the company’s new chief internal medicine officer to boost the company's efforts in obesity drug development.
  • The New York-based pharma giant said that List, who has done his post-doctoral research in the GLP-1 class of weight loss drugs, will oversee the company’s R&D work related to its upcoming cardiovascular, metabolic, hematologic, and obesity medicines.
  • List joins PFE from J&J’s (NYSE:JNJ) Janssen unit, where he served as global therapeutic area head for cardiovascular & metabolism.
  • The appointment comes as Pfizer (NYSE:PFE) is racing to grab a slice of the obesity drug market dominated by Eli Lilly (LLY) and Novo Nordisk (NVO), which, according to a recent research note from TD Cowen, are expected to maintain their duopoly until 2030.
  • In 2023, PFE discontinued a twice-daily regimen for its oral obesity therapy, danuglipron, citing side effects, and instead moved to advance a once-daily formulation into late-stage development.
  • At the recent earnings call, Pfizer’s (NYSE:PFE) chief scientific officer, Chris Boshoff, said that PFE remains on track to deliver a data update on the GLP-1 receptor agonist in Q1 2025.
